reviews.review_text:
[H1]“We’re trapped in a never-ending spiral of life and death”[/H1]

If a being can feel pain, fear, or loneliness, does it matter if it’s artificial ? is having a purpose enough to justify endless suffering ? can someone truly live if their entire existence is predetermined ?

[U]Story[/U]

The story follows android soldiers 2B, 9S, and A2 during a proxy war between humanity and machine lifeforms on a ruined Earth. Sounds simple enough — until the game slowly dismantles every assumption you have about good and evil, consciousness, emotions, and existence itself. What makes this game so memorable is that it never throws philosophy at you just to sound smart. The themes are woven naturally into the world and characters. Machines imitate human behavior despite not fully understanding it. Androids suppress emotions despite clearly having them. Everyone is searching for meaning in a war that may not even have meaning anymore. The storytelling reminded me a lot of Ergo Proxy, especially in how it handles identity and consciousness.
https://steamcommunity.com/sharedfiles/filedetails/?id=3701407843
[U]Visuals & Music[/U]

This isn’t the most graphically advanced game ever made, even for its time, but its artistic direction carries everything. The world is beautiful in a strangely lonely way. Abandoned cities overtaken by nature, empty amusement parks playing cheerful music with nobody truly happy inside, deserts swallowing forgotten ruins — every area feels melancholic, like the remains of a civilization whose meaning disappeared long ago. There’s a constant contrast between beauty and decay. The game can switch from peaceful silence to absolute chaos within seconds, and visually it always supports the emotional tone perfectly. The camera work deserves praise too. The soundtrack is what this game shines at the most. Every major moment hits harder because of it. Some tracks feel melancholic, others hauntingly beautiful, and a few almost spiritual. Few games understand how to use music emotionally the way NieR:Automata does.
https://steamcommunity.com/sharedfiles/filedetails/?id=3701407795
[U]Gameplay[/U]

The combat is smooth, fast, and stylish. Combos feel satisfying, dodging is responsive, and switching weapons mid-combat creates a great flow once you get comfortable. But what makes the gameplay memorable isn’t just the combat — it’s how many genres the game blends together. One moment you’re in a high-speed hack-and-slash fight, the next you’re playing a bullet-hell shooter, exploring an open area, hacking into enemy systems, or reading surprisingly emotional side quests. The gameplay variety mirrors the game’s themes perfectly: instability, repetition, and evolution. That said, the game isn’t flawless. Some side quests can feel repetitive, certain environments lack detail, and replaying sections for multiple endings may frustrate players who aren’t invested in the narrative. But unlike many games with multiple endings, these routes actually expand the story instead of simply changing small details. The first ending you get is basically just the beginning. And trust me, if you stop after the first ending, you have not finished the game yet.
https://steamcommunity.com/sharedfiles/filedetails/?id=3718612242
[B]Verdict[/B]

NieR:Automata is more than an action game. It is a dystopian philosophical masterpiece about identity, purpose, grief and what it actually means to be "alive". It’s one of those rare games where finishing it once means you’ve barely even started. It’s a melancholic existential journey disguised as one. It combines emotional storytelling, philosophical themes, experimental gameplay, and one of the best soundtracks in gaming into an experience that feels genuinely unforgettable. It’s not always perfect mechanically, and it occasionally hides its brilliance behind repetition, but when everything finally comes together, the payoff is incredible. Some games want you to feel powerful, NieR:Automata wants you to feel human. And somehow, a game about androids and machines accomplishes that better than most stories about actual people. I highly recommend this game — especially if you enjoy narrative-heavy experiences that stay with you long after the credits roll.

[B]RATING: 8/10[/B]

So I sit here, bewildered, after the game emotionally blackmailed me into deleting all of my save files for a gimmick. 80 hours of painstakingly achieved progress. Poof, gone. And I'm contemplating a clean new playthrough of both games. Because one question is burning itself through my mind. Did it all really come together so beautifully? 

It is kinda game that makes me happy to be a gamer. And even more happy to be a game developer. I want to run outside and scream at anyone who listens (or doesn't). See! I did not make it up, Games can be brilliant and innovative, there is so much more out there to discover, we don't have to settle for playing same tired game in a different skin over and over again, and endure same twenty year old gameplay design with sleeker animations. 

The game is effortlessly brilliant. Weaving together all the old school cool with cascade of innovations and subtle insightful twists. All the while juggling together gameplay mechanics that should never have worked together, nay should have exploded with chemical reaction, into one cohesive, beautifully mad melody. 

And the story. The game wields innocence and naivety as a deadly weapon, lulling you into false sense of security and then carving you up with emotional abuse and force feeds you existential dread through philosophical thematic resolutions. Oh, and there is a sprinkle of bitter sweet hope at the end, so you keep coming back for more. And it feels like there is always more to come back to. A whole new campaign opens up 40 hours in on playthrough #3. 

It's f*cking brilliant.

I have such complex feelings after finishing the game. Honestly, some parts like the camera shifts and Route B were a total pain, but the rest of the game is ♥♥♥♥♥♥♥ masterpiece level. I played through the whole game in one breath, the story, the music, and the atmosphere are just wonderful. 2B and 9S are characters I won’t forget anytime soon

I hope to find the time to come back to it again someday

In your 20s, there will be someone that tells you to play NieR:Automata. It is very important that you listen to them.

10/10 if you play it on Easy for the story. Easily the best combat in any video game, ever. Endings A-E are non-negotiable. Just play them and thank me later.
8.5/10 if you replay it on another difficulty. Slightly cumbersome controller mappings (why remove pod auto fire?) and resource tower repetition, backtracking wears you out if you play long enough.

Glory to Mankind

okay so i just finished this game and i need to write something down before the feeling fades.

i went in knowing it was "supposed to be good", i bought the game because my friend recommended it, i had seen the memes, the fanart, 2B everywhere on the internet. i thought i understood what i was getting into. i did not.

the first playthrough feels like a really solid action game with an interesting world. cool robots, cool vibes, some philosophy sprinkled around. fine. good. then you finish it and it tells you to play again and you groan a little because you're like, really? but you do it. and somewhere in Route B something shifts and you realize this game has been holding cards the entire time and it is just now showing them to you.

i got to 70% achievements naturally and then, look, i'm not ashamed, i bought the rest. some of those endings are just "die in a specific way 20 times" and i have a life, the game itself earned my completion playthrough, the grinding didn't need to. the actual story content i experienced legitimately and that's what matters.

after 40 hours i feel like i read a really good book that was also a really good game. it made me think about things i'm still thinking about.

9/10. the one missing point is for Route B pacing in the middle section which drags

It is sad having to say goodbye to this wonderful game, but it's part of the life cycle, right? 

We learn to love our heroes, their stories, and the world around this sad and cruel future world where we try to protect the last humanity. I have enjoyed every second of the game, and can’t let go of it just yet, but I must, it is worth replaying to receive the canon endings of the game, don't stop after ending A! There is so much more to unlock there even when first part of B story feels repeated, it slowly changes. Opening the reality of what is actually going on the earth and in the android society, I feel flabbergasted by the tales of humans and the androids. I really liked the different fighting abilities throughout the game, I glad you can change the weapons and pods gives extra help along the way + 9S hacking mini-games to defeat enemies was also fun way to attack enemies, then actually attacking them with a sword. I tried to achieve most of the side quests as possible, but some of them are boring or very dragging. I’m glad that there is transport areas to get to different sections faster, or use a moose to ride on to get to destination faster haha. (Minus, that I experienced often is that the game did struggle at time… mostly while playing as 9S, not sure why, but it lagged or just crashed which is annoying when the game is not AUTOSAVING!!!) Thankfully, you can change difficulty of the game all the time, so I changed to easy to go thought section as fast as possible on this moment, but mainly played at normal and only died 9 times! It isn’t as hard core soul like difficult, which I enjoyed, it had challenges and I managed it, which I’m very proud of myself for :D

I love the humour and interactions between android and robots in the game. Wonderful character cast and emotional soundtrack that managed to catch the atmosphere of the game!

Ending I have achieved: A B C D E K O W Z

"NieR: Automata" is a cool game with a distinct sci-fi aesthetic and direction that looks tailor-made for fans of dark sci-fi anime. Everyone else's mileage on how well certain genre quirks resonate may vary. The game's biggest strengths for me were the frenetic combat and, without spoiling anything, certain philosophical themes, as well as how the story often bled into the controls of the game itself. Maybe it's because I was a first-timer to this franchise, but despite being a rigorous player, the story still occasionally left me feeling shut out, like I'd missed some important emotional beats. "NieR" also has some strong idiosyncratic tendencies whose reasons weren't always obvious to me. These include the forcefully shifting camera angles, the most powerful enemy in many a hectic fight, and the way the game suddenly decides it's time for another unskippable mini-game. Unfortunately, the same care that so obviously went into the design and animation of the protagonists wasn't equally bestowed on the rather uninspired levels and enemy types. But for anyone who wants fast action with some heavy themes to brood over, "NieR" will probably scratch that itch.

[i] A future is not given to you. It is something you must take for yourself. [/i]

!!

A silly little game about androids until you play route c of doom and despair
10/10 ending e always makes me cry

This would be the best game ive ever played if it didn't run like ♥♥♥♥.

Still worth 10/10

Played through the entire series of DrakeNieR. Bought physical copies of DoD 1-3 since they aren't available on steam or digitally purchasable. It is a great series with a beautiful world. I am sad its over (so far)

Automata was a great ending to the journey. 
[spoiler] You are not alone. [/spoiler]

this game is truly life-changing because the first thing that happens is a super serious android in a blindfold shows up, lands from the sky like she’s late for work, and immediately starts committing war crimes against robots that are just trying to enjoy their existential crisis in peace

you think “okay cool hack and slash game” but then 20 minutes later you’re a tiny flying drone hacking into a robot’s brain while a melancholic soundtrack gently whispers in your soul like “hey btw nothing matters :)” and you’re like yeah fair enough

at one point i spent 3 hours doing side quests for a suspiciously happy machine who just wanted to learn what love is and i was like “sure buddy i’ll help you” and now i’m emotionally damaged

the game then suddenly switches genres like it’s going through an identity crisis worse than mine. one minute it’s a hack and slash, next minute it’s a bullet hell, next minute it’s top-down shooter, next minute it’s “congratulations you are now emotionally attached to a robot child please proceed”

also the game really said “do you want to feel pain?” and i clicked yes without reading the terms and conditions

then there’s the fishing mini game where you just stand there like a depressed fisherman in a post-apocalyptic world thinking about your life choices while catching literal scrap metal and sadness

and don’t even get me started on 2B’s storage space situation. she is carrying an entire arsenal under that skirt. military engineering has officially given up

overall 10/10 would get emotionally destroyed again, but this time with style, philosophy, and a very depressed robot named 9S who really needs a hug and possibly therapy
the only game allows you to purchase achievements with game currency, say goodbye to game chores, and enjoy the story and game design.

If you manage to push through the prologue and wrestle the controls into submission, you’ll discover one of the best games out there. Once it opens up, the whole experience just clicks: gameplay and storytelling are a solid 10/10. For the mankind… well, this is the kind of game that reminds you why we play in the first place.

One of the best games Square made. Amazing story, amazing combat. One of just 2 games with the free form fighting system, where everything you press does something in every situation. And the animations are cool.

Sadly some updates that were meant to make the game more performance friendly, related to Switch compatibility messed up LOD adjusting for some objects in specific parts of the game so they kinda break immersion a bit and you can't reverse that with even the highest quality settings. It's not too annoying but you might want to use mods anyway.

Fantastic. Worth doing 100%. 10/10

P.S.: This game has [spoiler]pervy achievements[/spoiler].

Very late to the party and really enjoyed this game after having it in my backlog for years! 
Once I got my head around chip slots and pods it was great fun to tinker with loadout and playstyle. I enjoyed the combat on controller. Music was fabulous throughout and enjoyed the characterisation and plot. 
I nearly stopped playing after ending A and found the route to ending B to be a bit dull as it is repetitive of ending A (not as long though). However it is extremely worthwhile to keep going through B (never give up!) to get to Endings C, D and E. This part of the game was my favourite.
I'm an older gamer, I play slow and like to explore and it took me 48 hours - not a completionist.
really enjoying it so far. very different game.  will give full review in the future, still finishing my 2B run

Just completed Ending A and now that im running through Ending B i can honestly say this game has alot to offer. You gotta keep going. There arent many games that are framed the same way Nier is. Highly Recommend. I feel like the first hour of game play i expererienced so many different forms of game style i was instantly drawn in. The story is also pretty damn good.

i will be honest here, i am not a big fan of square enix games(mainly cause of gameplay) other than xenogears, but still i will give it some credit it's story is very well made despite having ♥♥♥♥ ton of confusion, its ok if you dont understand it, u just have to live the experience. i remember yoko taro once said this story is basically evangelion retold. however i will say this story is much better written than evangelion(im not insulting evangelion im just saying my personal preference) mainly because 9S is more of a chad than shinji. AVOID THE SIDE QUESTS(speedstar questline especially). i used to think no one in square enix was worthy of remaking xenogears, however i think yoko taro might have a decent shot(of course, it should be done with takahashi's supervisation). glory to mankind

PKD would be proud. This hacking minigame has a great story/world and 10/10 OST! The actual combat is good! I don't replay games, but getting all 5 endings wasn't too bad. I HIGHLY recommend sticking with it and finishing Route B. Everything else follows easily.

If you haven't play it yet... Please do...
Don't spoil yourself with spoiler.
There's no game similar with this masterpiece.
Experience it yourself. You may give up in the middle, but you will pick it back up again.

The movement and combat feel amazing, this game is snappy and really fun to just play, there are even small details in movement that make going from place to place more fun, for example if you were to hit a bush in your way you'd slow down and have to sprint again to get back to your full speed which takes a few seconds even such a mundane part of the game has attention given to it, amazing game nothing else to say and I haven't encountered any problems playing it either.
personally this is the best game i've ever played. and i have played a TON of games, so ik what im talking about.
With all the 2b cameos in every other game. you'd think they would have made another by now. reboot the entire timeline or something. make all the endings a genjutsu or something.
Very different than stuff i'm used to but the weapon design, combat styles and the mix of hack n slash with side scrolling platformer is very unique and Im all for it.

An incredible story with a phenomenal soundtrack, striking visuals, and one of the most memorable endings in gaming history. However...
- Prologue doesn't teach you how to play the game and has no checkpoints (so if you die 30 min in you have to start over - hypothetically, didn't happen to me ofc).
- Forcing you to replay 60% of the same chapters (Route B) is a boring, lazy way to stretch play time. These weren't exactly the same but you have to put your time into it regardless.
- Most side quests are tedious, generic fetch quests.
- Combat mechanics and item gathering are shallow and repetitive. To defeat most enemies you can only mash one button and evade from time to time.

That said, the game is not that long, so if you have some spare time and you're not looking for a great combat system, go ahead and try it out. Feels a little like a good sci-fi/post-apo movie more than a compelling game.

Absolute cinema. A rollercoaster that impresses with its emotional story, fast paced combat, and excellent soundtrack. The credits just mark the beginning of your experience. Continue through ending E.

I have this game in my libary for a long time... first playthrough i liked it. But for some reason I only made it to the end of ending A. I let it lie for years. Then i played Nier replicant, finished all the endings and started automata right after again. Now after ending D I have to say:

10/10 

I  never gave such rating yet. You have to be ready for this game. Its emotional, it forces you to think, change your mind several times over each side fighting. And one last thing... The game really starts after ending B. 

If you havent played the game are at least bit of a philosopher and a romantic Just play it.... You will not regret it...

Nier:Automata, a game i just played in 2026 and not regret buying it. I personally avoiding any Nier content because I want to keep it for myself. Eventhough I was very late to play this game, but the feeling for playing this masterpiece is still there. The story is very touching, the music is amazing, the combat movement is flawless. Personal thought of this game is definitely worth to buy. Anyway thanks to anyone who help me in the very end of this game.
epic sauce robots and epic sauce story and epic sauce combat

I am writing this review straigh after my first time playing through it. NieR:Automata is not only the most impactful video game I have ever played, it might just be the most impactful piece of media I have ever interacted with, and that's with only a surface-level understanding of its meaning and its themes. Please. You owe it to yourself.

Way better than the predecessor, NieR Replicant.

The story is excellent, battles are fun and challenging, graphics are good.
I'm not a particular fan of the music tho.

Compared to Replicant they fixed alternate/extra endings, they are also very good and fun without being very repetitive.

It's really earned it's excellent reputation.
The soundtrack is an masterpiece! Still haven't finished it, but I'm already in love with the world and gameplay.

What is life? What is the meaning of our existence? These are just a couple questions amongst many that the game made me think about. The thought-provoking narrative, the gradual yet intriguing plot, masterful storytelling, the music that straight up hits the heart and amplifies the impact of the moment, the charming characters, the multi-genre combat, the artistic choices in visuals, almost everything was perfect in this game, while delivering one of the most profound gaming experiences. This is something I can't even classify as just a game, this is art.

The way the game exposes the player to the different sides of the story and drip feeds important lore through environmental interactions, character dialogue and flashbacks is wonderfully done. If you're on the first playthrough and thought there's not much happening, then YOU ARE NOT DONE WITH THE GAME YET! There are multiple routes which are necessary to fully comprehend the plot, so keep going (the game even tells you as much). 

The combat can be tough but fair, and is tons of fun given all the different weapons, moveset combos, and chip builds, not to mention certain character specific mechanics. The exploration on the other hand felt a little bit underwhelming, as I found myself thinking "huh, there's not much to do in this game except the quests". While the color pallete is quite bland and looks kinda blurry in a lot of areas, I think that is very much by design, further hammering in the theme and setting of the game into the player's head, which I came to appreciate later during my playthrough as I started understanding the intention behind the game. Towards the beginning of the game I was wondering why this game had such an insane reputation, as the plot and lore is very cryptic and slow at first, and it takes a while for things to get going, so the negatives kinda hit me first before really clicking with the game. The somewhat lacking exploration (at least for me) and the tedious grinding for some postgame stuff like max upgrades and hidden content are the only reason this isn't a straight up 10/10.

That being said, the game is definitely made to be played with a bit patience and an open mind and might not click for everyone immediately. But the reward for going through it all is sweet as hell and will change how you view and approach gaming. Solid 9/10.
All  around very  good game in  term of  story line, character  development, voice acting , amazing battle mechanics and of course the unforgettable soundtrack.

What a wonderfully strange, but compelling game. Relaxing yet action packed. A deep story or discovery for the main character 2B. Loved this game, and note to self completed ending A out of 27 ending.
very cool game lots of fun once you get use to the movement and the combat very well done

just finished 2B's storyline, music is amazing, worldbuilding is pretty great, i love the combat mechanics and switching between shoot em up, sidescroller, DMC style, etc. not too difficult either i fw this

The 47.5 hours I spent playing this game left more of an impact than the thousands spent in competitive/live service games. This game is truly a work of art. It goes on sale frequently but it's 100% worth the full price. Can't recommend it enough.
Came for the booty, stayed for the crippling existential dread
extremely good game, highly recomend to play the first game nier replicant before this one but is definitely one of my top favorite games of all time
Deep story passable gameplay. As it is made by Yoko Taro so play as many new game + you can, story is worth it.

Really great game, amazing game play! 

Had a bug for while where the game would freeze but the game would keep playing in the background which made me have to force close the game losing any unsaved progress. Took way to long to figure out turning off Vsync fixed the issue for me. Looks like this was a ongoing issue for many people.

Fun fun game but sometimes I'd get flashbanged with a closeup of 2b's thighs 
I like that you don't have to be an rpg guy to enjoy this one cuz grinding always felt optional, didn't need to spend 5 hours fighting filler enemies just to beat a boss like other really annoying games

The combat is stylish and fast paced. It feels hard at first, especially if you're not used to hack and slash games, but once you get used to it, it becomes really fun. It's not really Souls-like. The gameplay focuses more on smooth combat, movement, and combos.

There’s also a lot to grind if you want 100% achievements, so you can easily spend a long time playing it.

The side quests and NPCs are actually interesting too. Most of them have their own personality and stories instead of feeling like filler content.

The story is honestly one of the best parts of the game. There were multiple moments that completely caught me off guard. Just keep playing until Ending E. The first two playthroughs for Ending A and B can feel repetitive, but trust me, it becomes worth it later.

And the soundtrack... man. Every area has amazing background music. Some tracks are so good I just stopped moving for a while to listen to them.

One thing I hate though is getting jumped by a whole group of enemies. Also, you can consume drugs while fighting in this game.

Definitely recommended if you want a great singleplayer game with fun combat, amazing music, and a crazy good story

I really love that game , the story is just waw, the game as a lot of end (27 actually) but a lot of them are worth playing for!
This might just be the greatest game of all time, I've never felt so many emotions from a single game before, holy ♥♥♥♥ PEAK PEAK PEAK PEAK
10/10, otherwordly emotion packed into every scene, every line, and especially every track in the OST.

Hmmm... This is my first Nier game played all the way through. Ten years late to the Hype Train but I feel that I am able to appreciate more now than I would have before.

There's something about being presented a bleak and hopeless landscape, and being forced to find slivers of hope wherever possible.

I can feel that mirrored in my life almost exactly. Life is a struggle, and I feel that it is meant to be one. And it can be easy to lose hope in the face of those struggles, but, through our character, molded by experiences, we should be able to remain on the path we set out on.

"Life is a continuous stream of embarrassment." This hits very hard, especially as someone who can hang on to negative and embarrassing moments. But.. hopefully I can continue on struggling and perhaps make something of myself by the end of my cycle... perhaps I can be someone worth being proud of? Who knows?

Anyways, great game. 10/10 game play and story. The grind can get very tedious at the end, and the hidden super-boss was alright. I was grossly over-leveled by the time i fought him due to grinding for materials. But still a very enjoyable experience!
Bought for the goonbait, stayed for the existential philosophy. Amazing work, Yoko Taro.

Very good game, recommendable with a few notes (can't save for the first hour, no exit saves, full experience requires multiple "runs," significant portion is shmup/bullet hell).

The best word that describes this game is a "gimmick." A lot of the things that this game does are gimmicks, and your enjoyment really depends on whether they land or miss. To give a few examples: save games are explained in the story, but because of that you can not save at will, and do not even get "exit saves" (you must find a savepoint before quitting or lose progress); game "genre" constantly changes between a 3D action game, platformer and shmup/bullet hell; there are 27 endings of which 22 are jokes and the last "actual" ending is a gimmick (can't explain why without spoilers); the story is given over multiple "runs" with each further expanding the context of the story.
As far as I am concerned, the gimmick with the _story-telling_ (not the story itself, but the way in which it is told) is the most significant draw. Although it means that some content gets reused because one must do 3 full "runs" (+ some extra fights) to actually see everything, each one is distinct enough to be interesting. I am actually reluctant to use terms like "playthroughs" or "NG+" as they feel like part of the same "main story" that is broken apart by "endings," rather than alternative versions of the same story.
Combat is very comfortable as you get a remote attack option so you can just concentrate on dodging and only attack in melee when you feel ready. It is also very trivial to overlevel and make the game very easy, which can be quite satisfying when the game throws hordes of weaker enemies at you.

For negatives, the biggest ones are that the game was clearly designed for the controller (keyboard+mouse controls are mostly fine but can feel awkward/broken in the bullet hell sections), and that the inclusion of shmup/bullet hell genre is really questionable (it could be just me, but I think that bullet hell genre is "too far" from 3D action fighters for them to be in the same game; it is unlikely the same player will enjoy both genres).
The story kinda falls apart by the end, and in my opinion, it would have been much better off ending after the 2nd run (route B). The last run (route C) makes it feel like a depression galore that comes off as "depression for the sake of depression" rather than trying to make some bigger point.
The start is slow and somewhat demanding because the game needs to explain save games and fast travel through the main story so it takes some time before they are available. As a result you must do the ~1h tutorial in one sitting (and if you die you must restart), and if you go chasing side objectives before unlocking fast travel you will have to run around the level back and forth.

Overall, this game is a very unique experience that can be really enjoyable and memorable if its gimmicks work (for best experience, concentrate on the main story until you get fast travel).
Difficult sell on full price, but it usually goes on sale for like -60%.
DLC is highly optional (cosmetics + extra side story that must be unlocked through fighting. 2 of the 3 arenas are dumb slugfests that create difficulty via levels, and only the last one is kinda unique)

This was my second playthrough as I had already played it on release back in 2017.

This game is a masterpiece. The soundtrack is one of the best in gaming history (only E33 might be a bit better), the story makes you think about philosophical questions and has multiple unexpected emotional twists.

The gameplay is tight and stays fun throughout thanks to its constant variety in camera angles and bullet hell sections and the game still looks gorgeous even 9 years later. 

Oh and it plays perfectly on a handheld PC at a stable 60 fps. I cannot recommend this game enough.

You play though the game once just to find out it was the tutorial all along. 10/10 the game itself highly suggests you to play again, and you'll thank it for it. Several times.

This game is unique, if u r going to play this play replicant first to enjoy the whole emotional peaks plus the real game strats in 3rd act, YOKO is a genius

Came for the androids fighting robots. Stayed for the existential crisis.

This game gets better and better the deeper you go. It starts relatively simple, almost deceptively so, then slowly unfolds into something much bigger emotionally and philosophically. Definitely a slow burn, but in the best way.

The themes are incredible, the music is unreal, and Ending E completely justified the journey for me.

Don’t quit after the first credits roll.

♥♥♥♥♥♥♥♥♥, what an experience! Truly a modern classic, and it's no wonder few games try to replicate this experience (Stellar Blade) only to come up short.
The gameplay is never dull (perspective switches keep things fresh), multiple endings, story that starts out slow but keeps you pushing forward, and the OST is immaculate.

Highly recommended!
Nier Automata is a phenomenal game, the world is interesting, the characters are cool and fun. The Gameplay is quick and fun. Many fights are challenging and rewarding.

At first I was intimidated by the thought that there's multiple endings and that you can screw things up, but the game is really straight forward and wants you to play it.


I can definitely see why this game got very high praise. It's truly a good story told in the medium of a video game.

The combat feels solid and doesn't feel like a chore most of the time.

Soundtrack is really good to listen to and is nice to vibe to.

It's Peak, i don't even want to think about bad parts of the game, the ending was so peak i truly wish everyone to experience this beautiful world. This is forever one of the my favorites games that I've had played! "You think the Games just silly little Things!?"

I've never had a game have such a personal impact on me. The story, characters & lore are all incredible and had me invested at every moment. I'm still crying.

If you're reading this, remember to take care of yourself & those around you. You are loved, always <3 xx

Incredible music, story, and setting. The game blends philosophical themes with stylish action in a way that's uniquely its own. The multiple playthroughs reveal layers to the story that make everything click into place. Yoko Taro created something truly special here.

Absolutely amazing game, even if you dont like souls like / hack slash games thats not the purpose of this game
everyone should give this game a play
who knew clankers could make you feel like that

A great, touching narrative with wonderful music and lore, but ultimately has mediocre hack n' slack gameplay and terrible Difficulty selections. Normal mode is completely trivial with just spamming the same attack buttons and occasionally spamming dodge when enemies are going to attack while Hard mode turns all enemies into 1/2 shot machines with tiring amounts of HP. 
However the gameplay was serviceable enough that I wanted to continue to enjoy the story which was well worth it.

honestly F some segments (especially the one at the end of the game with switches every 2 seconds) but the story... the story is fantastic

also one thing I'd like to add: while I'm not very skilled with controllers, I think this game is not very comfortable to play with a controller. But that is sort of okay, because it's not very comfortable to play on the keyboard either

Amazing story and game, truly, very surreal and tragically beautiful. Not gonna lie, it pulled at my heartstrings constantly, especially mid-end game, and that final *final* ending bit? *chefs kiss to the developers 'and' players* (thanks for all your anonomys help, it was greatly appreciated. ::) 
I'm looking forward to playing Nier: Replicant now and watching the Nier: Automata anime!_!
10/10, arguably best story of any game i've played. Also very fun combat
Amazing graphics and fun game play.
An amazingly beautiful game which will change your outlook on life.

GLORY TO MANKIND!

i think the story is good enough to give this a thumbs up but a good story does not excuse bad pacing, having to play it over and over again to obtain more of the story, menial side quests, repetitive game play/combat, a tedious to navigate, small redundant map and a limited amount of enemy types. truly do not understand why this is revered by so many. highly overrated and a slog to get through but worth a shot for how creatively it was made i guess.

I would recommend this game to people that haven't played any action games or this title in general. Very fun, combat is super easy to learn, and the story is relatively great as well. But the game (in my opinion) gets a little repetitive after the first ending. Other than that very good game. 8.5/10, would recommend.
very interesting game with cool graphics but wish it could have native dlss/fsr or 120fps option but runs very well at 1440p at highest settings.

I haven't played this game in over 3 years, and since I've forgot to do ending E... i replayed it again to finish it.

I realized as well that i never reviewed it either! This game is one of my favorite games of ALL TIME.

The story is impeccable, the gameplay is even more impeccable, this was a meticulous craft of work that I will forever praise till I die, it is an absolute must play if anyone is ever curious on playing a Hack and Slash JRPG.
its slow at the start but man i got addicted to this game and got very sad when it ended so quickly

Great game with an amazing world and even better story and characters. The combat is sleek and fun, even if the enemy variety is poor. Additionally, the game never really rewards the player or creates any incentive to engage with its, albeit light, RPG mechanics, like its underdeveloped crafting system. The art direction in general though is exceptional and the UI's robotic style is equally memorable. It is also very well paced with great action set pieces and gameplay variety whether the standard hack and slash combat, the twin-stick shooter hacking, or the arcade-like flight sequences. Except for the Abandoned Factory sequence toward the end of ending A, nothing in this game overstayed its welcome; it does justify its ~20h runtime.

I'd strongly recommend this game to anyone who like story rich games because this game's characters, world, and themes are truly exceptional. This game truly is as great as everyone says, and the only people I wouldn't recommend this to are those that don't like darker and more melancholic stories.
Great story (and meta gameplay) dealing with pretty heavy themes ! Gameplay is a fun mix of hack and slash, with bullet hell and platforming.

An action-RPG game with an interesting storyline, beautiful characters, good music, simple battles, and character progression, and it's recommended to play the game multiple times to uncover the missing details of the storyline, but it's up to you.
I enjoyed the game because it's not boring and there's always something to do.
I recommend it to fans of action-RPG games.
2B and 2A are the best characters!

Soundtrack - 10/10
Story - 10/10
Art Direction - 10/10
Characters - 10/10
Endings - 26/10

literally a perfect game, don't skip this one.

Totally blown away with the scale and depth of this game. I bought it when new and had an episode where I couldn't enjoy single player games up until a few years ago. Finally got to this masterpiece and I'm disappointed in myself for not completing this when it was brand new, but I'm more than glad to finally experience it all. Collecting every achievement, every item, piece of gear and getting extremely lucky in the rng of plug-in chips for the amount of hours played made me feel extremely rewarded. 

Now to the unfortunate cons. I remember why I stopped playing this game when I first got it and it's from an extremely confusing screen freeze bug that requires you to fully close the game if you're nowhere near a save station. Every single solution that would fix it ended up not solving the issue. It felt like the game was fighting me every step forward taking away from the immersion and my overall enjoyment. It's not a fault of the gameplay or story, but the rather poor PC port. The 2021 final update that was supposed to fix a lot of these issues still didn't do it for me, so I turned to the mod scene that advertised patches for all these issues, yet STILL didn't resolve my issues. I will say it did allow the game to stay bug free for longer hours on average than vanilla for me, but once it would happen it would freeze even faster on the next opening of the game. It got so bad I coped that some apps open were the fault so I had to play with nothing open at all. 

I'll compile all the tweaks I did to make the game run more reliably here: Nvidia Control Panel v-sync on (the rest of the settings I have specific for my use case and not needed for other users), Deleted dx12 cache, Process Lasso cpu + gpu priority on high and cpu affinity to 0,2,4,6 cores only, ISLC keeping my memory in check, run game in Windows 8 compatibility and disable fullscreen optimization. 

As for mods I simply ran WAX, LODMOD, and some low impact texture mods.

This absolute is an absolute gem I shouldn't have slept on for as long as I did, and I hope if they do a remaster or a remake that the PC version isn't a mess like I had to go through to try and enjoy it reliably.

Amazing Story, the gameplay was unique (didn't enjoy 9S hacking but to each their own). I mostly played it for the story and what can I say it didn't disappoint

For me its an 9/10 game

The best story I have ever seen in a videogame. Absolutely beautifull. The gameplay is not perfect. the graphics aren´t perfect either, and many other things are not perfect, but playing this game will make a real difference from the moment before playing. The story, characters. storytelling, the places. the themes, the love. everything is just incredibly written it´s almost hard to believe. I had to give the story a 10/10. The game in general, a 9.7/10. Please give this a good chance. Thank you for reading.
the combat system has been really solid, the sroy after each and every main objective and quest is getting soo damn interesting, definitely recommend it to others based on how much ive played
I really enjoy the story and combat aspect of hacking and using the pods were really cool so yeah i recommend it to anyone who wants to get into the game. 10/10 ⚝

It's been 5 years since I played this game. when I watched the intro videos in the Steam, I recalled how good this game is. Very good story, and very good game plays. Nice characters with beautiful blade moves which for sure is the Mother of Stellar blade. Music is also good....
Not even half way through yet, but the cut scenes during certain quest are so good, and the soundtracks give an immersive feeling. <3

Bout the game espacially for the female lead  but in order to play the game I had to run as admin & make to to windows 8 for the compatibily & also tun off the one drive doc backup in order for the game to load on my laptop. After doing all that once the game was loaded it was awesome.

Nier: Automata is unlike any game I have played before. This is truly a game that borders on being a work of art.

My personal favorite aspect of the game has to be the OST. There are so many memorable tracks throughout the playthrough, many of which I will continue listening to long after finishing the game. The story is also one of the game's biggest strengths. It will have you thinking the entire way through, and once you reach the true ending, you may find yourself, as I did, reflecting deeply on its multi-layered storytelling.

The voice acting is also top-notch. Specifically, 9S’s voice actor, Kyle McCarley, does a phenomenal job conveying raw emotion on multiple occasions. The combat is satisfying and unique as well. So many different gameplay genres appear throughout the experience, and all of them are executed extremely well.

The few negatives that come to mind are mostly related to repetition, particularly during the pursuit of Ending B. I also feel the menu system could have been better thought out, as it can feel a bit unintuitive and confusing when you first start the game.

Overall, Nier: Automata is a special game that oozes authenticity and creativity. It is sure to leave a lasting impression and is a must play for anyone who enjoys rich narratives, great soundtracks, and fun action gameplay.

9/10

Absolute masterpiece, enjoyed every second of it and almost cried at some parts.

Obligatory 2B is hot end comment.

oh where do i start 
Story: 10/10
Gameplay 09/10
Graphics  8/10 
this is a kinda of game that blow your mind, and when you complete it, you set down and reflect on your life.

NieR Automata feels incredibly empty and incomplete without experiencing the other routes. I almost dropped the game out of laziness after hearing that there were 5 endings you would have to unlock to experience the full game, I don't regret doing them at all and to anyone else new to this game please do play through the other routes, it literally becomes a whole new game and story playing through them.

This game is really beautiful, the soundtrack, characters, world and designs are all amazing. Every character feels fleshed out and you really get to see their sides of the story and why they are the way they are, almost every character feels like a protagonist and I love how interactive this game is with the player and how it makes you feel immersed into the game, especially throughout the last ending. 

My only gripes with this game have to do with certain mechanics, such as the combat and chip system. I loved the hack and slash aspect of this game and it was what I looked forward to basically throughout the whole game, however it felt kind of shallow at times (personal opinion) compared to other games I've played, also it was just really easy on normal mode throughout the first route to the point where it became almost mind numbing and boring. Regarding the chip system, maybe I'm just too stupid but it was kind of confusing and I had no idea what I was doing up until the last route, this is probably attributed to my laziness so it shouldn't affect other peoples gameplay too much. Also to me, 2B and 9S' relationship didn't feel as fleshed out and as deep as I expected it to be, so I literally could not tell what their relationship exactly was.

On the last ending I didn't sacrifice my save data, I know thats kind of looked down upon but someday I want to come back to this game and look at my save data to remind me of everything and feel the same way I did playing this game, especially because I played this during an important period of my life. NieR Automata is a strong 8.5, bonus points for being the second media to make me cry like a b*tch in the same day.

Excellent world building, great music, very replayable for multiple endings, refined and stylistic combat, basically the gold standard of this specific genre. Iconic character design doesn't even need to be mentioned.
Great Story, gameplay that often swaps to entirely different mechanics / perspectives which adds a lot of variety combined with one of the best soundtracks I have listened to in a long time.

I can see the appeal of the game. Amazing story, good graphics and a little bit of existensial crisis. So far i only finished ending A and apparently ending K too by accident (damn mackerel). 2B is a great a character.. both narrative and design wise so does 9S and damn i love that dude. Well, i need to explore rest of the endings and then beating in a harder difficulty. Otherwise it's a good game, worth the money.
absolutely unparalleled, 100% recommend. only here can you "fail," overshooting the moon and landing on mars.
Beautiful, story sad but beautiful, combat one of the best, unique, music amazingly written. 10/10 will cry again.
Revisited this game since its launch date. Game still holds up. Great story, lore, soundtrack that keeps you absorbed.

I never understood why people said games can have a "soul" until now. NieR Automata is so unique. Unlike any other game I've ever played. I totally get how a game can have a "soul" now. You can feel the passion. This game has personality. It has such a memorable atmosphere. It's hard to believe this game was made in 3 years.

I tried Automata back in 2023, but only played 2hrs of it before quitting. I wasn't in the right mindset. I'm sooo glad I gave it another try. Nowadays is hard for me to focus on just 1 thing ie: 1 youtube video, anime, game, etc. 2 things that worked for me here were: 1) turn the volume up a couple levels to force my brain to focus on the game alone. 2) I fully committed to the game. I knew the game was special to a lot of people so I basically forced myself like "I'm going to like this game", but I didn't need to. Immediately I was having fun. Idk why it didn't click the first time I tried it.

Pros:
- Gameplay: actually super fun. The movement is super smooth. Combat is engaging. I love the perspective changes. Constantly keeping the game fresh. Also, love being able attack with my sword while shooting.
- Music: Certified classic. Probably top 3 imo. Behind Expedition 33. On par or better than Baldur's Gate 3.
-Story: I mean, c'mon! Everyone knows this. Incredible! Amazing plot twist and climax. Ending E had me crying in the club! I've never played a game with that ending structure and sequencing. I love how the title screen comes in after ending B. Also, very clever use of the settings menu with our characters being androids.
- Art Style: the graphics are very outdated, but the art style is so good you forget about it. Huge fan of the art of this game. Wallpaper machine!
- Characters: 2B is the real deal. The hype is real! Most crossovers for a reason. #2Booty. 9S and A2 are cool too.

Cons:
- I guess the graphics are outdated. Would love a remake by 2030.
- Not being able to look at full map before transporting.
- I wanted more of A2's backstory.
- Even though the payoff was amazing, I was getting frustrated with the pacing by the end. I was confused for so long. Maybe I'm just impatient.

10/10 would recommend. #7 on my favorites list. I heard I was supposed to play NieR Replicant before playing Automata. I'll be playing that in the nier future... Excited to try it out the "prequel".
i love this game but i encountered a glitch were the menu option selected will always go to the bottom and stay there.
Greatest OST of all time and astonishing lore, truly a masterpiece

I'M IN LOVE!
I had never tried the DrakenieR series before playing this game, but I always loved the character design and fashion. Then I bought this absolute gem on sale and I don't regret it at all! What I love about this game is that it's an open world and it's stories are super short. Where I'd normally be turned off by an open world game - NieR Automata keeps me engaged and immersed in the world.

 At the time of writing this, I've only beaten ending A, but I can definitely see me coming back in the future for the other endings as well - especially how short assumingly each of them are.

I love the gameplay! LOVE IT! It's a third-person shooter with hack-and-slash combat similar to Devil May Cry, and overall HUGE Destiny vibes with the weapons look and how you upgrade them! 

Last thing: if none of the above convinced you to play this absolutely beautiful game - at least play it for the sound track! Normally I'll play whatever game with YouTube on in the background, but every time a new song played in NieR Automata, EVERY TIME I would pause whatever I had on, and I would crank the volume on my laptop up to MAX just so I could hear whatever musical masterpiece Keiichi Okabe had cooked up!

I swear to god, only Square Enix know how to bring out the emotion in me! First Kingdom Hearts, then FF7 and 10 - now THIS! 

Can't wait to try out NieR Replicant in the future!!!
